title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
ellmer and friends

Bridging R and LLMs

Robust and designed specifically for easy interaction with LLMs directly from R
Broad provider support and meant for entreprise/production use
Allows models to extend their capabilities by executing R code

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
ellmer and friends

Bridging R and LLMs

Outputs are immediately usable in R
Integrations to ‘see’ the R session
Strong dev team

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
gander





More than a simple chat window
Powered by ellmer
RStudio & Positron


gander receives a snapshot of our environment with every request and recognizes what we are talking about, including object and variable names

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
gander demo
We use ellmer to specify which model powers the addin (and optionally set a system prompt).
Today we can use openAI or Anthropic models.

use with ctr+alt+G

ganderDemo.R
Setup instructions for Positron here

library(ellmer)
my_chat <- chat_openai() 
my_chat

text:

live_browser(my_chat)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Project-oriented workflow
Project: A folder on your computer that holds all the files relevant to a particular piece of work. We usually separate:
📁 Data
📁 Scripts
📁 Outputs





Jenny Bryan (2017)
Project-oriented workflow

Maëlle Salmon (2021)
Draw me a project

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Projects

Efficient and organized workflows
Relative file paths and working directories
Safer and cheaper approach when using AI assistants

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text: We’ll use cloud-based models for this course. More on this later.

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
API Keys
Working with APIs means we need to identify ourselves to the model provider and authenticate our encrypted connection to their servers

Overall workflow

Create and account with a model provider
💳 ?
Generate an API key
Keep it safe but reachable by your LLM tools

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
In R
Most packages work with keys stored in Environment Variables.
In GUI-based assistants
We enter our keys interactively

⚠️ Never share API keys in public repositories, network drives, shared documents or screen recordings.




H. Wickham  Managing secrets

Ted Laderas  A gRadual introduction to web APIs and JSON

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
API key setup
We’ll provide keys for two popular providers. These will only be active during the course hours.
R
Temporary: Sys.setenv(OPENAI_API_KEY = "api_key") (quoted) or “ANTHROPIC_API_KEY”
Permanent: usethis::edit_r_environ()

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
LLMs can help us with:

Text generation, translation, sentiment analysis, writing and debugging code, generating images and videos, etc.

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
section: Where are the models? Who makes them?
text:
Where are the models? Who makes them?
Many popular models live on the cloud

OpenAI (Known for GPT series including ChatGPT)
Google AI (Gemini models)
Anthropic (Claude models)
DeepSeek (DeepSeek R1)
GitHub Copilot (multiple models)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Models != Providers
Companies train and release models for different use cases

General purpose, lightweight models, coding, etc.
Models may be open or proprietary
Cloud providers may host multiple models from different makers
Web-based chats include models + various tools (which are not necessarily evident)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Let’s explore


LLM leaderboard

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Cloud-Hosted models



Relatively easy setup
Create account, set up billing if applicable, get an API key
Access to new and in-development models and massive computing power





Can be costly
Need internet access
We send our data to the API

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Local models
Download smaller models on our own hardware



Data not shared with a provider
No ongoing costs
Work offline





Steep learning curve
Large memory requirements
Slower performance



Examples:
Ollama (Simplifies running open-source LLMs locally)
Hugging Face transformers (lots of open-source models)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Which model do I use?
Considerations



Pricing
Free tiers, token pricing, billing policies
Context Windows
Compatibility with our chosen tools
Speed



Performance
Privacy
Hardware

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Context switching
Shifting our attention between different tasks or programs can be tiring and make us less productive and efficient.

Ask the chatbot in the browser
Copy the response and paste it in R
Share the errors or output with the chat
Repeat (and potentially introduce errors)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Coding with LLMs

Autocomplete


Models suggest ‘ghost text’ as we type

Very distracting during teaching

Suggestions may be unhelpful but mostly harmless

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Coding with LLMs

Chats


Send prompts, get answers
Often let us provide context and upload files
Results can be copied or inserted directly to a script
In the browser or through APIs

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Coding with LLMs

Agents/Agentic

Can run code and change files
Follow a general plan
Token-hungry and potentially slow
Makes us assume that results are correct

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
section: Questions we might put in an LLM chat window?
text:
Questions we might put in an LLM chat window?


> How do I add a subtitle to my ggplot figure?
> What are the arguments for pivot_wider()?
> I can’t join my table_1 object with my dat3 data frame, help!



Note the difference in the type of question. This will be important soon.

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Conversation with a chatbot
 🧍: name three mammals  🤖: dog, cat, mouse 

🧍: name three mammals  🤖: perro, gato, ratón

  What happened?

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
System Prompts
Define specific instructions on role, tone, or constraints

No system prompt?

Models default to base programming, leads to general and less predictable responses

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
System Prompts
Repeatable, consistent results suited to what we need to do.

“you are an expert R programmer that only uses base R”
“you are an expert R programmer that only uses base R. Do not add any comments. Do not explain the output”
“you are an expert R programmer. Comment the code in French”



ellmer prompt design Vignette

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text: LLMs are stateless

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
LLMs are stateless

LLMs don’t retain memory of prior interactions
Context happens by sending all previous messages with each new request
Because of limitations on tokens and context size, there are truncation and optimization strategies at work

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
LLMs are stateless


Start new chats often
Split up tasks across chats
Use detailed system prompts

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows

RStudio Sidebar (a new full-height pane for RStudio), available as of early Nov.
side package
Install a daily RStudio build from < 2 weeks ago. Enable sidebar using the command palette, run side::kick()

or

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Context awareness
Create these objects in your workspace, ask how to join them to keep all rows from df1 and matching rows from df2

library(tibble)
df1 <- tibble(id = 1:5)
df2 <- tibble(identifier = c(1, 1, 2), y = c("first", "second", "third"))

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Quarto?
An open source technical publishing system

Multi-format (PDF, HTML, .docx)
Execution of embedded code
Multi-output (books, blogs, reports, presentations)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Possum presentation and report
Let’s use Roo Code for this

Read the system prompts in the ‘mode settings’ for the extension
Have an LLM read the possums text file (possums.txt) and create a brief presentation (three or four slides)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Presentation exercise
Use LLMs to accomplish the following:

Create a css class with colors and text styling
Apply the class using Quarto fenced divs
Change the overall appearance of the presentation

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
Report

Use the .qmd file from the presentation to instead render an HTML report

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
section: Multiple inputs to usable R objects
text:
Multiple inputs to usable R objects
🐘 ellmer

Create chat object
Define scheme using a ‘type’ object
Run chat_structured

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
1. Biographical information

Create text string with biographical information
Desired schema using type_object
Use chat_structured method

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
library(ellmer)
chatbio <- chat_openai(model = "gpt-5-nano", system_prompt = "extract info")

bio_text <- "Gonzalo Pineda Reyes (born October 19, 1982) is a Mexican professional football manager and former player. Pineda played as a defensive midfielder for several clubs in Mexico, and also represented Mexico internationally. He last played in 2015 for the Seattle Sounders FC. He became assistant coach of the Sounders beginning with the 2017 season. "

bio_type <- type_object(
  name=type_string("First initial and last name"),
  sport=type_string("Sport played and professional leagues, separate with colon"),
  ctry=type_string("Country of origin")
)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
$name
[1] "G. Pineda"

$sport
[1] "Football: Liga MX, MLS"

$ctry
[1] "Mexico"

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
2. Messy tabular data
Send requests in parallel to work with vectors

visitorbook.tsv

parallelchat.R

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
3. OCR + extract
Bat PDF demo
Model comparison and features here



extractPDFdemo.R
stenoderma.PDF

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
4. Vision 🔭




Images can also be tokenized. Encode with content_image_* functions.


chatImg <- chat_openai("gpt-4.1-nano")
chatImg$chat(
  content_image_url("https://www.surfdogchampionships.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/08/i-Wg3VMHr-L.jpg"),
  "What is in this photo? be brief (1 sentence)"
)

title: Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
text:
4. Vision 🔭

Two dogs wearing orange life jackets are surfing together on a yellow surfboard
in the water.

Use ‘architect’ mode

WEEN 2025 - Integrating Large Language Model tools to our R workflows
All slides and materials live here. This one-day online course is organized by Physalia courses in conjunction with the The Wales Ecology & Evolution Network (WEEN) Conference on Monday, 24th November (the Monday following the conference).
This site may be updated after the course to correct any issues, or to add links or interesting topics discussed during the day.

📆   24 November, 2025
